= A صحب ṣaḥb, s.m. pl. (of ṣāḥib, q.v.), Companions, associates, &c. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

Coition; cohabitation. sohbat-dārī, yā sohbat k. , v. a. To cohabit. sohbat-yāftah , adj. Used to good society; well-bred. sohbateṅ uṭhānā, baratnā, yā dekhnā , v. n. To be brought up in good society ( āṅkheṅ dekhnā , 1, 2).

s. f. 1. Companionship, society. 2. An assembly, a fair. 3. Coition, cohabitation. 4. Discourse. صحبت برار ṣuḥbat-bar-ār, An acquaintance. صحبت براري ṣuḥbat-bar-ār-ī, Acquaintance. صحبت داري ṣuḥbat-dār-ī, s. f. Keeping company with, associating, acquaintance.
subs: s.m. 'One who carries on or promotes intercourse or companionship,' an acquaintance
s.f. Keeping company (with), associating; acquaintance; — sexual intercourse, coition
part. adj. Trained in, or used to, good society; well-bred
or ṣǒḥbateṅ baratnā, or ṣǒḥbateṅ dekhnā (-kī), To be brought up in good society; to be used to society.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صُحْبَت (suhbat), from Arabic صُحْبَة (ṣuḥba).

def: adj. Pleasing; charming ( khūb-sūrat ). Kachche khare suhāvne, au̇r gaddar kareṅ miṭhās, Samman! woh phal kaunse jo pakke se kaṛvās? The three stages of life. Unripe most lovely it appears, half-ripe its taste is sweet, But say what fruit is that which ripened turns to gall complete?
more: n. f. S. शोधनी A broom.

n. m. A hymn in praise of a goddess. Mātā ke sohle gā le!

more: 1. v. n. ( शोभन ) To become, to beseem. 2. v. a. ( शोधन ) To weed.

def: v.t. To clear, weed.

v. n. S. शोभन from शुभ to shine. 1. To suit; set off ( sajnā , 2). 2. To look beautiful or graceful. 1. Andhī āṅkh meṅ kājal sobhe, laṅgṛe pāoṅ meṅ jūtā! Can eye-paint brighten eyes both blind, or shoes the feet of lame ones grace? 2. Hathvā meṅ sobhe hari hari chuṛyā, tāhī par sobhe kanganvā! Wom. Marriage song.
s.f. (dialec.) An omen; augury.

def: s.m. Grief, sorrow, affliction, mourning, lamentation, repining, regretting

subs: (˚ka+ār˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Afflicted with grief or sorrow, sorrow-stricken, pained, grieved, sorrowful; — a very sorrowful person, &c.
(˚ka+āk˚), adj. (f. -ā), Overcome with sorrow, overwhelmed with grief, greatly distressed
s.m. Dissipation or removal of sorrow; — (f. -ā or -ī), a dissipator or remover of sorrow
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Whose principal occupation is grief; — a person filled with grief
adj. (f. -ī), Abounding in sorrow or woe

def: s.m. corr. of شوش śosh, q.v.
s.m. corr. of śoshaṇ, q.v.
v.t. To soak up, dry up, drink or suck up, absorb; to drink largely, to guzzle.
more: v. a. 1. To soak; suck up ( chūsnā 1,). Nārī nāhīṅ, nāharī nakh sikh setī khāë, Jīte sokhe rudhir ko, mue narak le jāë . Bihār Brindāban. No woman, but a tigress who devours you head to foot, Alive she 'll suck your blood complete, your soul to hell she 'll shoot. 2. To drink profusely; to quaff.
ex: جاؤ دھوپ میں کپڑے ڈال آؤ، کپڑا سوکھنے میں بھی تو ٹائم لگے گا
Go and put the clothes out in the sun, it’ll take time for them to dry too.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀇 (sukkhaï), from Sanskrit शुष्यति (śuṣyati). From the root शुष् (śuṣ).

def: s.f. Burning; — a revoke at cards; — 2˚ (part.), contrac. of soḵẖta, q.v.
more: (from سوختن) 1. adj. Burnt. 2. s. f. Burning; a revoke at cards.

ety: perf. part. of soḵẖtan; and = Zend śukhta, rt. śuć; S. शुचित, rt. शुच्
def: part. Burnt, scorched (Pers. plur. soḵẖtagān); — s.m. A slow match; tinder; a brand, torch; firewood, fuel
more: adj. Burnt; scorched. sokhtah-jāṅ, yā dil, Poet . adj. Heart-sore; grieved; love-sick. sokhtah , n. m. Wood fuel; charred cow-pats.

1. part. pass. (of سوختن) Burnt, scorched. (Pers. plur. سوختگان soḵẖtagān ). سوخته جان soḵẖta-jān or سوخته دل soḵẖta-dil, Inflamed in mind (with love or grief). 2. s. m. A slow match, tinder, a brand.
subs: soḵẖta-dil, adj. & s.m. 'Heart-consumed,' heart-sore, consumed with the fire of love, or grief, &c.; pained, grieved; love-sick; — grieved one; love-sick one.

def: s.f. Burning, combustion; a burn; vexation, heart-burning.
more: (from سوخته) s. f. 1. Burning, combustion. 2. Vexation, heart-burning.

def: inf. n. To burn, burning.
adj. Fit or de serving to be burnt.
more: adj. Fit or deserving to be burnt.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Full of grief, sorrowful, mourning, sad, grieved, distressed, afflicted.

s.m. (colloq.) = شولا śolā, q.v.

s.m. The aquatic plant Ӕschynomene aspera or Hedysarum lagenarium (a large perennial water-plant, abundant in tanks and marshes between Calcutta and Hardwār, with thick stems mainly composed of light white pith, which is made into floats, toys, artificial flowers, models of temples, &c., bottle-cases, stopples or corks, and hats, — anglice, 'Solar ṭopīs,' — invaluable as a protection against the sun).

ety: Prk. सोडहं, or सोलहं?; S. षोडशं
def: adj. Sixteen
more: adj. S. षोडश; P. shāṅzdah . Sixteen. Chhoṭī moṭī kāmnī sab hī bis kī bel, Bairī māreṅ dāṅo se, yeh māreṅ haṅs khel, Yeh māreṅ haṅs khel, tel phulel ramāveṅ, Kar solaḥ singār mān tapsī ke ḋhāveṅ . Kunḍlī. A fair maid small and plump, with poison in her breast, A foe in hiding slays, she slays with smile and jest, She slays with smile and jest, her hair oil-scented drest, Sixteen ways decked she bows the priest's obdurate crest. solah solah ganḍe sunānā , v. a. To abuse; call names.
subs: s.m. The game of hazard (in playing which several lines are drawn on the ground; — syn. soʼā-pāṭī)
(form. plur.), The whole sixteen; by sixteens, in groups of sixteen; sixteen times.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀮𑀳 (solaha).

def: s.f. A game played with sixteen cowries.

ety: solah, q.v.+Prk. अमओ, or अवँओ = S. तम+कः
def: adj. (f. -īṅ), Sixteenth.
v.t. To put to sleep, &c. ( = sulānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to sleep, to put to sleep; to hush, lull, quiet, calm, allay; to lay, repress, quell, subdue; to kill, murder
more: v. a. (caus. of سونا) To cause to sleep.

def: adj. & s.m. Causing or inducing sleep; — a sleeping-draught, an opiate.
adj. Sixteenth. solhaī , n. f. A game played with 16 kauṛis .

s.m. The moon-plant, Sarccstema viminalis, or Asclepias acida; the acid juice of this plant (prescribed to be drunk at sacrifices); nectar, the beverage of the gods; ambrosia; water; the moon; air, wind; camphor; rice-gruel; heaven, sky, ether; — name of a particular mountainous range; name of one of the eight guardian deities (lok-pāl) of the world; an epithet of Kuver; of Yam; and of Monday
more: n. 1. The moon. 2. A climbing plant from which a juice is extracted which is fermented and offered as a libation to the gods or to fire; Sercostamma .
subs: soma-pa, somap, s.m. One who drinks the Soma juice; a Soma sacrificer; the performer of a sacrifice; a Pitri of a particular class (said to be especially the progenitors of the Brāhmans); the manes of the Brāhmans
s.m. A vessel in which the Soma juice is kept
s.m. The drinking of the soma juice
s.m. A sort of grass, Saccharum cylindricum
s.m. A drinker of the Soma juice
som-tīrath, s.m. Name of a place of pilgrimage in the west of India
or som-rājī, s.m. The medicinal plant Serratula (or Vernonia) anthelmintica, black cumin-seed
s.m. The juice of the moon-plant, soma-juice

'Soma's lord, the divinity set up by Soma or the Moon'; name of a celebrated Linga, i.e. columnar emblem of Śiva or Mahādev, or of the place or temple where it was set up (this temple was established in the town of Somnāth-pattan, and was one of the twelve celebrated Linga temples which, in various parts of India, are held in especial veneration; it was so famed for its splendour and enormous wealth, that it attracted the attention of the celebrated Maḥmūd of Gaznī, who, in A.D. 1024, under pretext of destroying its idols, carried off its treasures along with its renowned gates)
s.m. Name of a town on the western coast of India, in the peninsula of Kattywār and province of Gujerāt (it was celebrated for the temple of Somnāth above described)
s.f. The last day of a dark fortnight falling on a Monday (celebrated as a great festival and bathing-day)
or soma-vallarī, and soma-vallikā, s.f. The moon-plant, Sarcostema viminalis
s.f. The moon-plant; the shrub Cocculus cordifolius; the medicinal plant Vernonia anthelmintica
vulg. som-yagya, s.m. A soma sacrifice, an offering or libation of the juice of the acid A sclepias.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سوم (siwum).

def: Somalia (a country in East Africa, in the Horn of Africa)
wety: Borrowed from English Somalia. Spelling affected by Arabic صُومَال (ṣūmāl).

def: s.m. = som-wār, q.v.s.v. som.
s. m. Black cumin seed. (Serratula anthelmintica: Roxb. ).
n. m. Monday; the day of the moon. somvat'ī, samotī māvash , n. f. The last day of the dark fortnight on a Monday, celebrated as a great festival and bathing day.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सोमवार (somavāra, “moon-day”).

def: adj. = sā, 'like'; — 2˚ Emphat. particle = hī, q.v. insep. prefix, With, together with, along with; having, possessing, accompanied by, &c. (it is combined with nouns to form adjectives and adverbs).

s.m. A sort of red sugar-cane; the plant Bignonia indica; the river Sone (which rises in the district of Nāgpūr, on the table-land of Amarkanṭak, four or five miles east of the source of the Nerbudda, and running first northerly and then easterly for 500 miles, falls into the Ganges above Patna)
subs: and son-nadī, s.f. The river Sone.

def: adj. & s.m. Red, crimson; red in the face (from passion, &c.); — the colour red or crimson; fire; a sort of red sugar-cane; blood; red-lead; the plant Bignonia indica; the river Sone (see سون son)
subs: s.m. or śoṇ-bhadrā, s.f. The river Sone
s.m. A red gem, a ruby.

ety: i.q. soṅh = Prk. सवहं, or सवहो; S. शपथं, or शपथः
def: s.f. An oath, asseveration (syn. saugand; qasam)
more: s. m. 1. Name of a river. 2. Name of a flower. (Bignonia indica).
postpos. From, &c.: see H. سي se.
subs: (-kī), To take an oath, to swear (by).

ety: Prk. सुंतो, the substitute for the abl. plur. of the S.
def: postpn. (dialec.) = se, q.v. The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. Its sound in Arabic is that of th in the English thin; but in Hindūstānī it has the sound of s. According to the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 500.

ety: so˚ = Prk. सोव(इ), or सुव(इ) = S. स्वप(ति), rt. स्वप्
def: v.n. To sleep; to lie down, repose recline; to die; to lie (with, -ke-sāth), to cohabit (with)
subs: v.n. (intens.) To go to sleep, to fall asleep
v.n. To keep or remain asleep; to go to sleep ( = so-jānā); to be asleep.
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

ety: Prk. सुवणअं, or सोणअं; S. सुवर्ण+कं
def: s.m. Gold
more: n. m. S. स्वर्ण, , Pr. sonam . Gold. 1. Sonā jhonā zāt nahīṅ hai . Prov. Gold is not caste. 2. Sone se ghaṛāvan maiṅhgī . Prov. The workmanship is dearer than the gold. (It costs more than it is worth). 3. Sone kā gaṛvā, aur pītal kī peṅdī! Prov. A pitcker of gold, the bottom brass. 4. Sone ko salām, rūpe ko alek, bhūke ko na dekh . Prov. To gold he bows, to silver nods, the hungry he cant see. 5. Sone ke sehre biyāh ho! Benediction. 6. Sonā le-ke miṭṭi bhī nahīṅ detā . Prov. Gold he takes but gives not even clay. 7. Sonā khonā aur pānā donoṅ bure . Prov. Gold found or lost both evils are. 8. Sonā chhūe to miṭṭī ho jāë . Prov. At his touch gold turns to earth. (An unlucky man). 9. Maṭṭī chhue to sonā ho jāë . Prov. Whatever he touches (earth) turns to gold. (A lucky man). sonā-chāṅdī . 1. Gold and silver. 2. Riches; wealth; bullion. sonā sugandh , adj. lit . pure as gold. Pure; innocent.…

v. n. S. स्वप or शी to sleep; Pr. sutto asleep. 1. To sleep. 1. Dushman soë na sone de . Prov. The enemy nor sleeps himself nor lets you sleep. 2. Ke sove rājā kā pūt, ke sove jogī abdhūt . Prov. Saying. Or royal child hath sleep's repose, or calm ascetic only knows. 3. Dūjā pahrā rain kā sab sove sansār, Ke koī jāge bhogiyā, ke koī dukhiyā nār . Dohā. Now in the second watch of night the world is locked in sleep, But sensualists and suffering women sole nightvigils keep. 4. Bole ke, na chāle ke, maiṅ to sūte ke bhalī! E. Prov. No good for talk or company, I'm the one for sleep! (The daughter-in-law). 5. So jā, lalvā! ro mat, bīrā, bahuaṛ kāman lā dūṅgī! Rus. Lorī . Lullaby. Sleep, my child! dont, brother, cry! a pretty wife for you I'll bring! 6. Bahut sonā diladdar kī nishānī hai . Prov. Much sleep is the forerunner of Poverty. 7. Soyā muā barābar . Prov. Sleep is like death. 8. Sūt hī…
1. ( शयन ) v. n. To sleep; ( met. ) to die. 2. or in Braj saunā ( स्वर्ण ) s. m. Gold. سوني کا نوالا sone kā nawālā, An expensive banquet, a delicious morsel.
subs: s.f. Gold and silver; riches, wealth; bullion
adj. (f. -ī), Like gold; of a golden hue
adj. & s.m. 'Sweet or pure as gold'; pure, spotless, innocent; — a man of high birth or of great ability
s.m. A delicious morsel; dainty food; an expensive banquet
s.f. 'A golden bird'; a rich person; a fat prize
or son-mākhī, s.f. Gold-stone; marcasite; iron pyrites.
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

def: adj. Of gold, &c. ( = sunahrā, q.v.)
more: adj. Golden. سونہلا پاني sonahlā pānī, s. m. Gilding.

def: adj. Of gold, &c. ( = sunahrā, q.v.)
s.m. = سنار sunār, q.v. s.m. A worker in gold, a goldsmith (also written sonār)
subs: s.m. The goldsmith's quarter (of a town).

def: s.f. = sunāran, q.v. s.f. A goldsmith's wife; a woman of the goldsmith caste.

adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = soṅdhā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Sweet-smelling, fragrant, having a strong odour or perfume; having the smell of a new earthen vessel when first wetted; smelling like parched pulse, or fried potatoes, &c.; — s.m. Fragrant smell, perfume; a composition of fragrant substances used for washing the hair; pomatum, pomander
s. f. (dakh.) Fragrance: see سوندهاهٿ.

adj. (f. -ī), Sweet-smelling, fragrant, having a strong odour or perfume; having the smell of a new earthen vessel when first wetted; smelling like parched pulse, or fried potatoes, &c.; — s.m. Fragrant smell, perfume; a composition of fragrant substances used for washing the hair; pomatum, pomander
more: adj. S. सुगन्ध Pr. suandh . Sweet-smelling; fragrant, as a new earthen vessel (when first wetted), parched pulse, potatoes, marshmelon, etc. Pāchan to kachriyāṅ haiṅ sauṅdhī aur mīṭhiyāṅ! ( Phūṭ ). Delhī street cry. Marshmelons a digestive good, fragrant, and sweet, ho! soṅdhā , n. m. A fragrant hair-wash.
s. m. 1. A composition of fragrant substances used for washing the hair. 2. Smelling like that of a new earthen vessel when wetted or from parching of pulse.

ety: soṅdhā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. वट्टी = S. वृत्तिः

def: adj. Faithful, believing; — s.m. A kind of funeral rite or ceremony in honour of the departed spirits of deceased relatives observed at various fixed periods and on occasions of rejoicing as well as mourning (it consists of offerings with water and fire to the gods and manes, and of food and gifts to the relatives present and to the Brāhmaṇs assisting; and is believed essential to the ascent of departed spirits to a world appropriated to the manes and to their residence there); the gifts presented on the occasion of the observance of the ceremony described above
subs: s.m. Any god presiding over funeral rites; a Viśva-dev; Yam (lord of the dead); Manu Vaivasvat (brother of Yam)
s.m. An epithet of Manu Vaivasvat ( = śrāddh-dev).
